steps.title: Balance cost and inconvenience
steps.content: Calculation Mode aims to minimize financial cost and personal inconvenience. Participants using it still chose standard plans even when green plans were cheaper. Since lower price alone did not lead them to choose the green plan, the likely issue is the plan's cap on electricity usage, which would create inconvenience.

steps.title: Reject noncalculation claims
steps.content: Choice A contradicts Calculation Mode by saying savings were not factored in. Choice B shifts to social responsibility, a Role Mode concern rather than a cost-and-convenience concern. Choice D claims participants doubted cost-effectiveness, but the passage says the green option was cheaper. Choice C explains that the usage cap imposed burdens that outweighed the savings. The answer is C.
